title: Warships pilot names and predictions Asuka Rei Mari
display_title: Warships pilot names and predictions Asuka Rei Mari
wikitext:
[]: Warships, pilot names and predictions - Asuka, Rei, Mari             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Thu Dec 30, 2010 9:14 am I've recently been looking at names of warships. There are four related to Asuka: - Graf Zeppelin (name carried by Asuka's mother in the NGE series) German WWII-era aircraft carrier that was never completed during the war, and never operational. It disappeared after the war. Decades later, it became clear it had been seized and subsequently sunk by Soviet forces, in anticipation of the Cold War. This reflects Kyoko's story, of a promising force for the Evangelion project, only to be severely (mentally) damaged before Unit-02 ever became operational, and wallowing in a mental hospital until meeting a pitiful end due to her own inability to function. - Langley (name carried by both NGE-Asuka and Rebuild-Asuka) Name of two WWII-era US aircraft carriers. The first Langley was also the first US aircraft carrier in operation. It was severely damaged by the Japanese in 1942, and sunk by US forces because it was deemed unsalvageable. The second Langley was commissioned in 1943 and carried on the name and tradition of its predecessor. It remained in operation until well after the war. We see in NGE and EOE how Asuka enters as a powerful Evangelion pilot, but succumbs to insecurity and mental trauma. Only to reappear in time for the final, where she once again soundly beat the enemies. (What happened after the enemies regrouped is echoed by the next name.) Rebuild-Asuka still has the name Langley, so I expect a full return to awesomeness in the remaining movies (short-lived or otherwise). - Soryu (name carried by both NGE-Asuka and her mother) Japanese WWII-era aircraft carrier that took part in the attack on Pearl Harbor, and was sunk at the battle of Midway. Soryu was the fastest aircraft carrier in the world at the time of her launch. (Soryu is also the name of a class of Japanese submarines, but these did not exist at the time NGE was produced in 1994-1997.) This name implies great military victories, followed by total military defeat. Because both NGE-Asuka and her mother Kyoko share it, it reflects their joint fate in their final battle in EoE: first tearing the MPEs to pieces, only to be torn to pieces by them themselves. (Ironically, the MPEs can be seen as a metaphor for Americans.) Shikinami (name carried by Rebuild-Asuka) Two Japanese destroyer warships. The first Shikinami was the second of the Ayanami subclass of the Fubuki class of destroyers, in operation in the period 1929-1944. It saw much action during WWII, and was sunk by a US submarine. The second Shikinami was the third commissioned of the Ayanami class of destroyers in 1958, and remained in operation for nearly 30 years before being decommissioned. What does this tell us of Rebuild-Asuka? Looking at the first Shikinami 's operational life, it saw lots of action, including several of the big battles. This reflects Asuka's track record against the 7th and 8th Angels. Being sank by a submarine can be likened to the 9th Angel attack on Unit-03, as the attack was invisible until it happened. However, there was a second Shikinami , one which did not get destroyed in action. Therefore, Asuka will be back, and I believe she will also make it to the end of the movie series. In fact, Asuka Langley Shikinami's names predict a better fate than Asuka Langley Soryu's names do. --- Bonus Section: Other names not associated with Asuka Part I: Rei Ayanami We already saw the name "Ayanami" pop up twice. There were two series of destroyer warships named after it, both of which had a Shikinami . However, both series were named after the first warship in their class: Ayanami . However, there was also an earlier Ayanami , a WWI-era Kamikaze-class destroyer. The first Ayanami was launched in 1909 and was nearly obsolete from this very first moment. She remained in operation for 30 years, but was renamed in 1928, to free the name for the second Ayanami . Does this story remind you of both Rei I and Unit-00? The second Ayanami was launched in 1928, took part in WWII naval battles, mostly in supporting roles, and was finally sunk in 1942 after being heavily damaged in a successful naval battle. The captain survived. This is Rei II, of course. The third Ayanami was launched in 1957, and remained in operation for 30 years. It was not involved in any war or naval battles. Here we have Rei III, who did not pilot an Evangelion. Personally, I believe Rebuild will have only one (significant) Rei, and from that perspective I expect the most recent Ayanami to reflect Rebuild-Rei (i.e. clear sailing all through the movie series), while the WWII-era Ayanami reflects NGE-Rei (i.e. got sunk in the End (of Evangelion)). Part II: Mari Illustrious Makinami Then there is Makinami . There have been three Japanese destroyers carrying this name. The first was a WWII-vessel of the Yugumo-series, which was sunk in 1943, only a year after being launched. The second was the last of the second Ayanami series, completed in 1960 and decommissioned in 1990. The third Makinami is a destroyer launched in 2004 and currently still in operation. The first Makinami reminds me of Provisional Unit-05, the second Makinami is probably a reference to Mari piloting Unit-02, which is still operational (although heavily damaged) after the 10th Angel battle. It seems highly probably that Mari will pilot again, and will probably receive one of the newest Evangelion units that will be introduced in the future movies. And finally, Illustrious . This was a name carried by no less than five warships in the Royal British Navy, from the period 1789 until the present. The first was the only to be lost at sea, as it sank during a storm after it was heavily damaged in battle, and was being towed for repairs. The last two were aircraft carriers (and the fifth is still operational). What does this say about Mari? It's everyone's guess, but I'm guessing she will pilot three different EVA Units, and will pilot an EVA in five battles. Additionally, she will survive until the end.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Thu Dec 30, 2010 10:25 am Intresting Parallels in the battle Records... It might have been very intentional, as Anno was once a military geek.

[]: Re: Warships, pilot names and predictions - Asuka, Rei, Mari             Quote Post by die-yng » Fri Dec 31, 2010 2:59 pm Thanks @Jornophelanthas. for that very informative post. I'm always careful not to attach to much intention to such details, because more often than not, you find out that the writer/creator (no matter if book or television or movies) didn't go as far with his own designs as fans or critics suspect. There is a story about a famous author who after having been enlightened by literature critics, what his new book was trying to do, what his intentions were, what it was about etc.. Simply said: "It's a bloody book! It just tells a story!" Of course it would be cool if all the names had been picked in regards of the ships histories.

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Fri Dec 31, 2010 3:43 pm Wow, 100+ views and only positive comments. I must be doing something right. One thing that surely is no coincidence is that Asuka Langley Soryu, who has been named after no less than three aircraft carriers is first spotted aboard an aircraft carrier. And said aircraft carrier also carries Unit-02. Asuka Langley Shikinami was spotted immediately shortly after the 7th Angel blew up an entire fleet of destroyers in one movement. (For the record, both Shikinamis were destroyers.) This raises a question with me. What, if any, symbolic meaning could be found in the distinction between destroyers and aircraft carriers in the pilot names? (Please refer to my original post for a better overview of which name refers to which ship type.)

[]:             Quote Post by die-yng » Fri Dec 31, 2010 4:11 pm The obvious of course would be to see the aircraft carriers as more important, as they form the backbone of every modern fleet and already did during the last century. Meaning a battlegroup is usually buitl around and led by an aircraft carrier. The role of destroyers has changed a lot, up to and for the most part of WWII destroyers were small, easy to manufacture warships, whose purpose was to form a protective envelpoe around the heavier cruisers and battleships or aircraft carriers. Today they are pretty much the biggest standard warship, with only the US and the british navy even having higher classes of vessels. But since the Ships seem to be mostly oder models, the premier role would probably apply here. I'm not so sure wether you can really devise anything from that, but at least in the case of A. Sohryu, you could relate it to her role as Shinji's "other" and the part she plays in the final ending of NGE (meaning of course EoE). The question is wether the characters roles were already so "laid down" when it came to naming them.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri Dec 31, 2010 4:30 pm Jornophelanthas wrote: Wow, 100+ views and only positive comments. I must be doing something right. One thing that surely is no coincidence is that Asuka Langley Soryu, who has been named after no less than three aircraft carriers is first spotted aboard an aircraft carrier. And said aircraft carrier also carries Unit-02. Asuka Langley Shikinami was spotted immediately shortly after the 7th Angel blew up an entire fleet of destroyers in one movement. (For the record, both Shikinamis were destroyers.) This raises a question with me. What, if any, symbolic meaning could be found in the distinction between destroyers and aircraft carriers in the pilot names? (Please refer to my original post for a better overview of which name refers to which ship type.)

[]:             Quote Post by die-yng » Fri Dec 31, 2010 5:22 pm Kendrix wrote: The names must have come pretty late in the developement/conception of the characters - Rei's was meant to lure some former Sailor Moon producer in. (!) Perhaps he had no ideas for the Names and decided on a theme naming - they're all hommages and/or ship names. I experienced a similar thing but wasn't as creative as Anno - It resulted in a "Ruth Luther" living in the same town as a "Clerith Jovanka" as I picked the Names on a whim. XD

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Dec 31, 2010 5:27 pm Kendrix wrote: The names must have come pretty late in the developement/conception of the characters - Rei's was meant to lure some former Sailor Moon producer in. (!)

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Fri Dec 31, 2010 5:31 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: IF the girls' naming schemes relates to their fates in the films, I think this means that Mari will indeed get Eva-07 since it wasn't mentioned in the Q Preview (And Shinji gets Eva-08 as theorized)... But who knows at this point.

[]:             Quote Post by die-yng » Fri Dec 31, 2010 5:33 pm Azathoth wrote: I still don't think Unit 07 is any kind of normal Eva. They felt perfectly comfortable with revealing Unit 08; why not Unit 07? If it isn't Mari, there's something else weird about it; or else it explodes or is otherwise useless; or it's something else that would completely blow Q wide open if we saw it.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Fri Dec 31, 2010 5:42 pm die-yng wrote: That rings true.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Dec 31, 2010 8:21 pm Azathoth wrote: Or, I neglected to mention, it could just be a case of Khara not having designed it yet - which certainly rings true with general production methods for NME so far.

[]:             Quote Post by facespace » Fri Dec 31, 2010 9:44 pm Azathoth wrote: I still don't think Unit 07 is any kind of normal Eva. They felt perfectly comfortable with revealing Unit 08; why not Unit 07? If it isn't Mari, there's something else weird about it; or else it explodes or is otherwise useless; or it's something else that would completely blow Q wide open if we saw it.

[]:             Quote Post by Nonoriri » Fri Dec 31, 2010 9:58 pm facespace wrote: Wait... When the hell did they announce a unit 08? From the q trailer I assumed that I only saw unit 6.

[]:             Quote Post by facespace » Fri Dec 31, 2010 10:20 pm Wow I was dumb it never clicked that they were different. That's pretty sick, but I am gonna miss the mass produced evas from EoE...

[]:             Quote Post by Assasinate » Sat Jan 01, 2011 8:23 am i hope in the next movie there will be 2 or more EVA's show up

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Jan 01, 2011 9:10 am EVA 08 has the third eye!

[]:             Quote Post by Mad » Sat Jan 01, 2011 4:14 pm Nonoriri wrote: ... right here

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sun Jan 02, 2011 10:23 am Assasinate wrote: i hope in the next movie there will be 2 or more EVA's show up

[]:             Quote Post by die-yng » Sun Jan 02, 2011 12:38 pm So.... is anybody actually going to present some ideas as to what the new or changed names might mean???
